text: Continuous integration, or CI, is a software development practice where developers regularly combine their code changes into a central repository. A key part of CI involves automated builds, which compile the new code into executable software automatically. After each build, tools also run a series of tests to check if the code works correctly. The main goal of CI is catching issues early, before they become bigger problems. These tasks are managed through CI pipelines, which are automated workflows that define the steps to build, test, and validate the code. By catching bugs soon after changes are made, CI helps development teams save time and maintain higher software quality.
